title: Wapol Returns to His Kingdom
description: On pages 12–15, wapol returns to his kingdom as wapol comes back to reclaim his kingdom as Luffy, Sanji, and Nami prepare for the dangerous climb toward Dr. Kureha.
scene: Wapol Returns to His Kingdom. Wapol comes back to reclaim his kingdom as Luffy, Sanji, and Nami prepare for the dangerous climb toward Dr. Kureha. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It seems we've got quite the cruel father.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Looks like it's coming from here...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: master of doctors D.R. KUREHA age 139

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Dr.kureha's beloved Reindeer Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Tony Chong Chopper

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: You want to know the secret of my youth!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: to think that's actually a 140 year-old granny...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Oh? And are you a doctor?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But you forgot about how much your hands hurt, didn't you? Hee hee hee!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The hell are you doing to my son!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Fine, I'll stop. Happy, pappy? Now stay still for a moment.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I only lightly touched his leg but his limbs seem to be in quite a bit of pain.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No fever but looks like we've got a pyogenic inflammation on the leg.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Must be a bacterial infection in the bones.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: W-What's Wrong With My Boy, Doctor?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: This disease can still be treated while in its early phase.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: but he was on the verge of death.

summary: This part centers on wapol returns to his kingdom, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
